ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder and Rowena Navia for Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS").
Rebecca Paul Director of Fiduciary Services for JFCS.

The Court questions whether bond in the parent trust will cover the subtrusts. Ms. Navia stated that there should be a new bond, that they can petition for a bond after they do distribution once they get the order, and that she hopes to have distribution made within two weeks of the order being filed. Mr. Feder stated that JFCS does not routinely request the 1% guideline fee, but it will when warranted, and that they did a good job in getting things in order and maintaining things. Mr. Feder also explained the level of services JFCS provided, spoke of their level of expertise, the level of difficulty and complexity of the Trust with respect to tenants, allocation of subtrusts to minimize the taxes, etc., stated that they put a lot of thought and analysis into it, that there still is a significant amount of work to do, that the 1% is based on a 5 million dollar estate, that the initial size of the estate was 8.5 million and that all of this should be taken into account. The Court points out that on top of the 1% fees, there are additional costs for the accounting, the property manager and the investment services and asks why they shouldn't be deducted from the 1% fee. The First and Final Account and report of Successor Trustee, etc. is taken under submission. Status on filing of bond is set for December 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder & Rowena Navia for Jewish Family & Children's Services(JFCS), represented by Rebecca Paul.

The Court stated that this is a second and final accounting, that there are a couple of issues that were raised in the examiner's notes, the first is whether it makes sense to allocate to the sub-trusts and the fees being requested in the trust for conservatorship activities. Mr. Feder stated that the allocation is important due to the tax consequences, that the case is complicated, that there are residuary gifts, that there are assets that appreciated at different rates, that they do not anticipate federal or state taxes and that the issue is capital gains taxes making the allocation critical. Mr. Feder addressed the fee question stating that there were a small number of hours for the conservatorship included in the trust accounting, that JFCS structured one manager for the trust and one for the conservatorship, that the manager for the trust dealt with the big picture, that there were overlapping issues such as the transfer of property from the conservatorship to the trust, that in a perfect world it would have been billed in the conservatorship, that because the trust and the conservatorship were being accounted for at different times it was difficult to catch and that the entries referencing the conservatorship were dealing with issues for both the conservatorship and the trust. The Court stated that there is an entry for 4 hours spent on conservatorship statements. Mr. Feder replied that not all of the hours were for both. Ms. Navia stated that they were also asked by counsel for Diane Jones to provide statements for her accounting which was done in conjunction with the other accounting and that it was parallel but not duplicative. The Court asked about hours specifically aimed at statements for the conservatorship. Ms. Navia stated that it was a lot of work to obtain statements for the trust accounting while at the same time obtaining statements for the conservatorship, that it is a very complex and difficult case and that JFCS has worked really hard. Mr. Feder stated that it would be different if the trustee's fees were not subject to court approval, that there are many people interested in this, that there have been no objections, that the fees requested are on an hourly basis and reasonable and that JFCS should not be penalized. The Court took the accounting petition under submission and stated that it will take a look at any reduction in fees as appropriate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS"). Rebecca Paul from JFCS also present.

Ms. Paul reported that the Conservatee is relatively stable, that he is responding well to the care that is being provided and that he is happy to be at home. Mr. Feder stated that the current estimated value of the School Street property is $544,000.00. The Court questioned if a $200,000.00 repair is worth it. Mr. Feder stated that the problem is that the property is specifically bequested, that a small interest (1%) is owned by Mr. Garadis, that the property is vacant, that the Tenants were evicted for non-payment and that the bid for $82,000.00 is attached to the declaration. The Petition for Authority to Conduct and Pay for Maintenance and Repairs to Trust Real Properties, etc. was taken under submission.
